description: Fix three visual issues in the side panel UI: (1) improve tool group header visual hierarchy so group labels are clearly distinguishable from tool rows, (2) fix the dark theme's low-contrast appearance by increasing border brightness and background depth, and (3) replace the active border animation's smooth interpolation with a stepped blink to eliminate the muddy olive/brown third color caused by sRGB interpolation between yellow and black.

userStories.title: Add left accent bar to tool group headers for clear visual hierarchy
userStories.description: Add a primary-colored left border accent to tool group header dividers in both PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard so group labels are immediately distinguishable from tool rows.
acceptanceCriteria: Group header divs in PluginCard.tsx have a left border accent using the primary color
Group header divs in BrowserToolsCard.tsx have the same left border accent treatment
The group header background is visually distinct from the tool row alternating tint (even:bg-muted/10)
Both PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard use identical group header styling
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx (group header styling) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx (same group header styling) Context: Both files render tool group headers identically. The current group header markup in PluginCard.tsx (line 237): ```tsx
{group.name}
``` The same pattern appears in BrowserToolsCard.tsx (line 162): ```tsx
{group.name}
``` The problem: `bg-muted/20` is barely distinguishable from tool rows that use `even:bg-muted/10` alternating tint. The group header lacks enough visual weight to read as a structural separator — it looks like a slightly different tool row. Changes: Add a left border accent bar using the primary color to create a strong visual rail that the eye follows to identify sections. Change the group header className in both files from: ``` border-border border-b bg-muted/20 px-3 py-1 ``` to: ``` border-border border-b border-l-2 border-l-primary bg-muted/20 px-3 py-1 ``` The `border-l-2 border-l-primary` adds a 2px yellow left accent bar. This is a small change with high visual impact — it leverages the primary yellow as a structural marker. The eye immediately reads 'section break' without needing to parse the text. Also consider increasing the background tint slightly from `bg-muted/20` to `bg-muted/30` to further differentiate from tool row tint, but only if the accent bar alone doesn't feel sufficient. Constraints: - Do NOT change the font, text size, or uppercase styling of the group name — only the container div's border/background - Both PluginCard.tsx and BrowserToolsCard.tsx MUST have identical group header styling - Do NOT modify tool-groups.ts or ToolRow.tsx - Do NOT change the Accordion component or its border styling

userStories.title: Improve dark theme contrast by adjusting border and background values
userStories.description: Increase the dark theme's visual contrast by brightening the border color, deepening the background, and optionally adjusting the card color — restoring the crisp neo-brutalist look that works in light mode but is lost in dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark --border value is brighter than the current #5c5c5c (at least #707070 or higher)
Dark --background value is deeper than the current #1a1a1a (at most #141414 or lower)
The flat offset shadow (4px 4px 0 0 var(--border)) is clearly visible on dark cards
Both platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css and docs/app/global.css have identical dark theme token values
Dark avatar palette colors remain readable against the new background/card values

userStories.title: Replace smooth active border animation with stepped blink to eliminate muddy interpolation
userStories.description: Change the activity-border-flash animation from a smooth ease-in-out interpolation to a stepped binary blink between yellow and border color, eliminating the olive/brown third color caused by sRGB interpolation between yellow (#ffdb33) and black (#000000).
acceptanceCriteria: The activity-border-flash animation never shows an intermediate color between yellow and the border color
The animation uses CSS steps() timing or discrete keyframe stops to prevent color interpolation
The activity-border-fade-out animation also avoids producing muddy intermediate colors during the fade-from-yellow transition
Both PluginIcon and ToolIcon active states use the updated animation
